title: RSVP Responses Button, Nav Text Contrast Fix & Mobile Music Controller Fix

requirements.summary: Add a prominent 'View RSVP Responses' button on invitation cards in /dashboard and /dashboard/:slug that opens a modal displaying all RSVP submissions with guest details, attendance status, and summary statistics
acceptanceCriteria: A visually distinct 'View RSVP Responses' button appears on each invitation card on the /dashboard page
The same button appears inside the invitation editor at /dashboard/:slug
Clicking the button opens a modal or expandable panel showing all RSVP submissions
The panel displays Guest Name, Phone, Attending badge (Yes=green/No=red), Guest Count, Message, and Submitted At
A summary row shows total responses, total confirmed guests, and total declined
An elegant empty state is shown when no RSVPs have been submitted
The button uses a premium gold/crimson accent color distinct from Edit, Preview, Publish, and Delete buttons
Data is fetched using the existing getRSVPsByInvitation React Query hook pattern

file_operations.description: Create a new modal component that displays RSVP responses for a selected invitation. Use Shadcn Dialog component as the base. Fetch data using useRSVPsByInvitation and useRSVPsStats hooks from useQueries.ts. Display responses in a table or card list showing Guest Name, Phone, Attending status (Yes badge in green, No badge in red), Guest Count, Message, and formatted Submitted At date. Include a summary section at the top showing total responses, total confirmed guests, and total declined from RSVPStats. Show an empty state with decorative icon when no responses exist. Style with luxury gold/ivory theme matching the app aesthetic.

Add a 'View RSVP Responses' button to each invitation card. Style it prominently with gold/crimson accent (bg-gradient-to-r from-gold-400 to-crimson-600) to distinguish it from Edit, Preview, Publish, and Delete buttons. Position it in the card actions area. Add state to track which invitation's RSVPs are being viewed and render the RSVPResponsesModal component, passing the selected invitationId. Use existing invitation data structure to pass invitation.id to the modal.

Add a 'View RSVP Responses' button in a prominent location within the editor interface (e.g., in the header area or as a new tab/section). Style consistently with the dashboard button using gold/crimson gradient. Add state management to control the RSVPResponsesModal visibility. Import and render RSVPResponsesModal, passing the current invitation's ID from the route params. Ensure the button is visible regardless of which tab (Couple Details, Events, Template & Theme, Media) is active.

Verify that useRSVPsByInvitation and useRSVPsStats hooks are properly exported and functional. If they don't exist, add React Query hooks that call getRSVPsByInvitation(invitationId) and getRSVPsStats(invitationId) backend methods. Use the existing query pattern with proper query keys (e.g., ['rsvps', invitationId] and ['rsvp-stats', invitationId]). Ensure hooks return loading, error, and data states appropriately.

requirements.summary: Fix navigation link text visibility in the Header component by applying light text colors (ivory/white/gold) in the transparent header state and ensuring readability across all backgrounds with proper contrast treatments
acceptanceCriteria: 'Home' and 'Dashboard' nav link text is clearly legible against the landing page hero background in both transparent and scrolled header states
Navigation links use a light color (ivory/white or gold) in the transparent header state
Text remains readable after the header gains a background on scroll
If a backdrop or boundary treatment is used, it is consistent with the luxury aesthetic
Hover states are visible and use appropriate gold/blush accent colors
Fix applies on both desktop and mobile navigation

file_operations.description: Fix navigation link text visibility by applying proper text colors based on scroll state. When isScrolled is false (transparent header at top), apply text-ivory-100 or text-gold-200 to all nav links ('Home', 'Dashboard', 'Create Invitation' button text) to ensure visibility against dark/image backgrounds. When isScrolled is true (header with background), ensure text uses sufficient contrast (text-slate-800 or similar). Update hover states to use gold/blush accent colors (hover:text-gold-400) that remain visible in both states. For mobile menu, ensure the same color treatment applies to the hamburger icon and menu items. Consider adding a subtle text-shadow or semi-transparent backdrop (backdrop-blur-sm with bg-black/20) to nav items in transparent state for guaranteed readability across all background images.

requirements.summary: Fix the MusicController component to work on mobile devices by ensuring proper touch event handling, sufficient touch target size, and correct audio playback after user gesture to respect mobile autoplay restrictions
acceptanceCriteria: The floating music controller button is tappable on mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Android Chrome)
Tapping the floating button opens/closes the music controller panel on mobile
The touch target for the button is at least 44x44px
Audio plays after the user taps the play button on mobile (respects mobile autoplay restrictions by requiring user gesture)
Play/pause state toggles correctly on repeated taps
The controller remains functional and visible on small screens without layout overflow
Music controller continues to work on desktop/laptop as before

file_operations.description: Fix mobile functionality by ensuring all interactive elements use onClick handlers (which work for both touch and mouse events) instead of any mouse-specific events. Verify the floating button has a minimum size of 44x44px (w-11 h-11 or larger, and add p-3 for comfortable tap target). Ensure the audio element respects mobile autoplay restrictions by only calling audio.play() in response to direct user interaction (inside onClick handlers). Add explicit touch-action: manipulation CSS to prevent double-tap zoom. Test that isPlaying state updates correctly on tap and that the play() promise is handled with proper error catching for mobile browsers. Ensure the expanded controller panel doesn't overflow on small screens and remains accessible. Consider adding user-select-none to prevent text selection on repeated taps. Verify volume controls work with touch events.
